remote.title: Outside home Wi‑Fi
remote.body: Telegram clients on mobile data need your public IP/DDNS and a forwarded TCP proxy port. Keep the web panel on LAN only.
remote.steps1: Keenetic → Network rules / Port forwarding → TCP
remote.steps2: Forward the proxy port (default 8443) to the router itself
remote.steps3: Paste public IP or DDNS into the share host field above
remote.steps4: Do not forward UI port 7788 to the internet

update.title: Updates
update.restarting: Installing… the panel will reconnect in ~20s

share.title: Connect in Telegram
share.hint: Public host / IP for QR and links

web.title: Panel access
web.note: API is protected by this login (no browser Basic Auth popups). Leave empty to open the panel on LAN.

nfqws.title: Works with nfqws2
nfqws.body: Listen-only proxy: no NFQUEUE, iptables or packet marks — safe beside nfqws2-keenetic.
